mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with month-over-month FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM moves in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60d returns with a t-statistic > 1.5 over any 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output that immediately pressures equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 70% of observations following negative INDPRO MoM changes during post-shock recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate combined with stable or declining CPIAUCSL indicates real rate compression that boosts equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines while CPIAUCSL MoM change <= 0.1%
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of triggered cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and corporate revenue potential, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show a negative or zero correlation with PAYEMS MoM growth over a 5-year rolling window, with Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP growth signals an economic contraction that immediately triggers risk-off positioning, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5d returns in > 65% of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below 1.0%, or event study alpha is positive and significant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of low inflation signals robust supply-side expansion that supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of observations when INDPRO accelerates during low-inflation periods, or regime-specific IC is not significantly positive.
